# americancloud_dns_record (Resource)

A DNS record within a zone. Any change replaces the record (the API has no stable record id). Manage one record value per resource.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "americancloud_dns_zone" "example" {
  name = "example.com"
}

# A record
resource "americancloud_dns_record" "www" {
  zone_id = americancloud_dns_zone.example.id
  name    = "www"
  type    = "A"
  content = "203.0.113.10"
  ttl     = 3600
}

# MX record (priority required)
resource "americancloud_dns_record" "mx" {
  zone_id  = americancloud_dns_zone.example.id
  name     = "@"
  type     = "MX"
  content  = "mail.example.com"
  priority = 10
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `content` (String) Record value: an IP for A/AAAA, a hostname for CNAME/NS/MX, or text for TXT.
- `name` (String) Hostname relative to the zone. Use `@` for the zone apex.
- `type` (String) Record type: one of A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, NS, SRV, TXT.
- `zone_id` (String) DNS zone identifier (from `americancloud_dns_zone`).

### Optional

- `port` (Number) Port. Required for SRV records.
- `priority` (Number) Priority. Required for MX and SRV records.
- `ttl` (Number) Time to live in seconds (60–86400). Omit to use the zone default.
- `weight` (Number) Weight. Required for SRV records.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Synthetic identifier: `zoneId/name/type/content`.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# DNS records have no server-side id; import by composite id zoneId/name/type/content.
terraform import americancloud_dns_record.www "11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111/www/A/203.0.113.10"
```
